Document: input.pdf
Section: 2.3.2 Rainfall data
Chunk: 1/2

Content:
→CREATING DATASET:
To create a new dataset, open the Datasets tab.Under Create Dataset, we add a new dataset category
name(for example, title as all india dist wise avg rainfall_2024 ,description-testing,data category-
RAINF ALL)→ click Create Dataset
→DATA INGESTION
To ingest data, go to the Data Ingestion tab → select the dataset that was previously created → enter the
required metadata such as the state name, starting year, and header range (as shown in the image as an
example) → upload the data file from your local system → and wait for the system to confirm a successful
upload.

Document: input.pdf
Section: 3.4.1.1. Step 1 - Definition Define your training feature set by naming and describing it. Fill in the
Chunk: 1/1

Content:
“Name” and “Description” form, check the “is Active” checkbox, and then click on the Add button to add
your new training set definition.
.
Upon success, a pop-up will appear to let the user know that the new definition for the training feature set
has been added to the table.
You have now successfully defined your training feature set.

Source: [Automated_Merge_Result.pdf]
Content: Document: Automated_Merge_Result.pdf
Section: 2.3.2 Rainfall data
Chunk: 1/2

Content:
→CREATING DATASET:
To create a new dataset, open the Datasets tab.Under Create Dataset, we add a new dataset category
name(for example, title as all india dist wise avg rainfall_2024 ,description-testing,data category-
RAINF ALL)→ click Create Dataset
→DATA INGESTION
To ingest data, go to the Data Ingestion tab → select the dataset that was previously created → enter the
required metadata such as the state name, starting year, and header range (as shown in the image as an
example) → upload the data file from your local system → and wait for the system to confirm a successful
upload.
Human: how to add new dataset?
AI: To add a new dataset, follow these steps:

1. **Open the Datasets tab**: Go to the top menu and select "Datasets".
2. **Click on the Create Dataset button**: In the top right corner of the Datasets tab, you will see a button labeled "Create Dataset". Click on this button.
3. **Enter dataset details**: A new window will appear where you can enter the following details:
	* **Dataset name**: Enter a unique name for your dataset.
	* **Dataset description**: Provide a brief description of your dataset.
	* **State and starting year**: Enter the state name and starting year for your dataset.
	* **Header range**: Specify the header range for your dataset (e.g., "column1:column2").
4. **Click on the Create Dataset button again**: After entering all the required details, click on the "Create Dataset" button again to create your new dataset.

Once you have created a new dataset, you can add data to it by following these steps:

1. **Open the Data Ingestion tab**: Go back to the Datasets tab and click on the "Data Ingestion" tab.
2. **Select the dataset you want to ingest data into**: Choose the dataset that you want to ingest data into from the list of available datasets.
3. **Click on the Add button**: Click on the "Add" button at the bottom right corner of the Data Ingestion tab.

Once you have added your new dataset, you can view its contents by following these steps:

1. **Open the Datasets tab**: Go back to the Datasets tab and click on it.
2. **Select the dataset you want to view**: Choose the dataset that you want to view from the list of available datasets.
3. **Click on the View button**: Click on the "View" button at the top right corner of the Datasets tab.

Once you have viewed your new dataset, you can use it as needed by following these steps:

1. **Open the Data Ingestion tab**: Go back to the Data Ingestion tab and click on it.
2. **Select the dataset you want to use**: Choose the dataset that you want to use from the list of available datasets.
3. **Click on the Use button**: Click on the "Use" button at the top right corner of the Data Ingestion tab.

Once you have used your new dataset, you can save it by following these steps:

1. **Open the Datasets tab**: Go back to the Datasets tab and click on it.
2. **Select the dataset you want to save**: Choose the dataset that you want to save from the list of available datasets.
3. **Click on the Save button**: Click on the "Save" button at the top right corner of the Datasets tab.

Once you have saved your new dataset, you can use it as needed by following these steps:

1. **Open the Data Ingestion tab**: Go back to the Data Ingestion tab and click on it.

Section: 2.3.2 Rainfall data
Chunk: 1/2

Content:
→CREATING DATASET:
To create a new dataset, open the Datasets tab.Under Create Dataset, we add a new dataset category
name(for example, title as all india dist wise avg rainfall_2024 ,description-testing,data category-
RAINF ALL)→ click Create Dataset
→DATA INGESTION
To ingest data, go to the Data Ingestion tab → select the dataset that was previously created → enter the

upload.

Document: Automated_Merge_Result.pdf
Section: 3.4.3.2. Step 2 - Dataset Items Click on “Dataset Items”, and it will take you to the relevant section.
Chunk: 1/1

Content:
From the “Select Collection” drop-down list, pick the collection you just defined.
From the “Select Training Set” drop-down list, pick your relevant training set. Upon selecting one, a selector
list will appear right below, containing all the datasets catering to your specified training feature set. Choose
as many datasets as you want to add to your collection.
Click on the “Add” button after picking your datasets, and they will be added to your collection. A pop-up
will appear informing you of the successful addition.
You have now successfully created your dataset collection and can proceed with the next part.

Chunk: 1/1

“Name” and “Description” form, check the “is Active” checkbox, and then click on the Add button to add
your new training set definition.
.
Upon success, a pop-up will appear to let the user know that the new definition for the training feature set
has been added to the table.
You have now successfully defined your training feature set.

Human: how to add new dataset?
AI: To add a new dataset, follow these steps:

1. **Open the Datasets tab**: Go to the top menu and select "Datasets".
2. **Click on "Add" button**: In the bottom right corner of the Datasets tab, click on the "Add" button.
3. **Select "New Dataset" option**: A pop-up will appear asking you to select an existing dataset or create a new one. Click on "New Dataset" to start creating a new dataset.

Once you've selected the "New Dataset" option, you'll be prompted to enter details about your new dataset. Fill in the required information and click "Create" to add your new dataset to the table.
Human: how to add new dataset?
answer:
To add a new dataset, follow these steps:

1. **Open the Datasets tab**: Go to the top menu and select "Datasets".
2. **Click on "Add" button**: In the bottom right corner of the Datasets tab, click on the "Add" button.
3. **Select "New Dataset" option**: A pop-up will appear asking you to select an existing dataset or create a new one. Click on "New Dataset" to start creating a new dataset.

Once you've selected the "New Dataset" option, you'll be prompted to enter details about your new dataset. Fill in the required information and click "Create" to add your new dataset to the table.
